head.breadcrumb: Step 1 of 2 · Tell us about your project
head.lede: We read your answers before the call, so the time gets spent on your problem instead of intake. Should take about two minutes.

trustBanner.heading: What we collect, and don't
trustBanner.body: **This form does not ask for an email or phone number.** The questions below help us understand your project — that's all. Your answers come to us through our scheduling tool only after you book a time.
trustBanner.nuance: Heads up: the booking step uses Calendly, which needs your name and email to send the calendar invite. That's between you and Calendly — we don't store, sell, or use your email for marketing. No newsletter, no follow-up sequence, no list. If we don't end up working together, we move on.

steps.title: Tell us about the project
steps.body: The form below — about two minutes. We use it to figure out if we're a fit before either of us spends time on a call.

steps.title: Book a 30-minute call
steps.body: You'll land on our scheduling page next. Pick any time that works. We'll confirm by email through Calendly.

steps.title: Hear back, either way
steps.body: If we're a fit, we'll send a written proposal within a few days. If we're not, we'll tell you on the call and point you somewhere better.

form.label: 02 · Project details
form.title: The questions.
form.submitNote: **Next:** you'll be sent to our Calendly page where you can pick a time. Your answers above will travel with you so we have them when we meet.

questions.label: What kind of engagement are you looking for?
questions.help: Pick the closest fit. We can adjust on the call.

options.title: Build something
options.sub: A custom AI-powered application or internal tool

options.title: Discovery
options.sub: Figure out where AI fits in your business, and what to build first

options.title: Review
options.sub: A second pair of eyes on something you (or another team) built

options.title: Not sure yet
options.sub: Help me figure out what I need

questions.label: What are you trying to solve, or trying to build?
questions.help: A few sentences is plenty. The clearer this is, the better we can prepare.
questions.placeholder: e.g., Our team manually processes about 200 vendor invoices a week. We want to extract line-item data automatically and flag anything unusual for review...

questions.label: What's your rough budget?
questions.help: Honest answer is best. Helps us suggest a realistic shape for the work.

options.title: Not sure
options.sub: Want to discuss on the call

questions.label: About your company
questions.help: Skip if you'd rather share on the call.

questions.label: How did you hear about us?
questions.help: Helps us figure out what's actually working.
